nameMeanings.name: Armstrong
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (mainly Northumberland and the Scottish Borders): nickname from Middle English arm + strang, for someone who was ‘strong in the arm’.
Irish (Ulster): adopted as an English equivalent of Gaelic Ó Labhradha Tréan ‘strong O'Lavery’ or Mac Thréinfhir, literally ‘son of the strong man’.

History: This surname was brought to PA, NJ, and NH in the 18th century by several different families of northern Irish and northern English Protestants. One such was James Armstrong, who emigrated from Fermanagh to Cumberland County, PA, in 1745; another was John Armstrong (1720–95), who settled in Carlisle, PA, c. 1748.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese (Ōi): written 大井 ‘large well’. It is found in central and eastern Japan.
Japanese (Ōi): written 多 ‘many’. It was pronounced Oho in ancient times and is listed in the Shinsen shōjiroku. It is not common in modern Japan.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 黃, see   Huang   1.

variants.name: Huang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Wei, Ming, Jian, Ying, Yi, Jing, Wen, Jin, Ping, Min, Yong, Cheng, Hong, Chong, Chang, Pao, Sha, Shen, Tun, You, Chung, Shiu, Ti, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 黄, meaning ‘yellow’ in Chinese: (i) from Huang (黃), the name of an ancient state (present-day Huangchuan in Henan province)annexed by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) traced back to some minority ethnic groups in ancient southern China.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Armstrong of Blackness was born in 1278, in Scotland. She had at least 1 daughter with James Douglas of Hermiston and Lothian. She died in 1337, in her hometown, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Armstrong of Blackness est née en 1278, à Écosse. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec James Douglas of Hermiston and Lothian. Elle est décédée en 1337, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Armstrong of Blackness wurde 1278 in Scotland geboren. Sie hatte mit James Douglas of Hermiston and Lothian mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1337 im Alter von 59 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Armstrong of Blackness è nata il nel 1278 nel Scotland. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con James Douglas of Hermiston and Lothian. Morì nel 1337, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland: スコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラス

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Armstrong of Blackness(은)는 1278년 스컷랜드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 James Douglas of Hermiston and Lothian(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1337년 그녀의 고향에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Armstrong of Blackness nasceu em 1278, em Scotland. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com James Douglas of Hermiston and Lothian. Ela faleceu em 1337, em sua cidade natal, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Armstrong of Blackness родилась в 1278 г. в местности Шотландия. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от James Douglas of Hermiston and Lothian. Она умерла в 1337 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 59 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Armstrong of Blackness nació en 1278, en Scotland. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con James Douglas of Hermiston and Lothian. Murió en 1337, en su pueblo, a la edad de 59 años.
